english: to seem / to appear / to shine (of the sun or light)
notes: Has two distinct meanings: (1) to seem/appear (used with adjective or infinitive clause), (2) to shine (used for the sun, moon, or light). Both are common.

sentences.german: Er scheint sehr müde zu sein.
sentences.english: He seems to be very tired.

sentences.german: Die Sonne hat den ganzen Tag geschienen.
Das Problem scheint gelöst zu sein.
sentences.english: The problem appears to be solved.

cloze_text:
Er {{c1::scheint}} sehr müde zu sein.
Die Sonne hat den ganzen Tag {{c2::geschienen}}.
Gestern {{c3::schien}} die Sonne den ganzen Tag.

cloze_sentences.english: He seems to be very tired.

cloze_sentences.text: Die Sonne hat den ganzen Tag {{c1::geschienen}}.
Gestern {{c1::schien}} die Sonne den ganzen Tag.
cloze_sentences.english: Yesterday the sun shone all day.

cloze_sentences.text: Das Projekt {{c1::scheint}} ein Erfolg zu sein.
cloze_sentences.english: The project seems to be a success.
